rulings.Case Title: IRENE CARTER VS. TAD NGUYEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 17, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS TAD NGUYEN, and, KIM NGUYEN DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer to 5th and 6th causes of action sustained without leave to amend. No extreme or outrageous conduct alleged. No pattern of continuous encroachment with 23 year gap between first and second alleged incidents. No demonstration of malicious conduct. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE CARTER VS. TAD NGUYEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 17, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS TAD NGUYEN, and, KIM NGUYEN MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted without leave to amend for reasons stated in the ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBOOSTOFF & KALKIN A PROFESSIONAL LAW COOPERATION VS. RON BOCHNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Dismiss Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 17, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT RON BOCHNER Motion to Dismiss Complaint. Is denied. The policy behind the mandatory fee arbitration statutes, to alleviate the disparity in bargaining power in attorney fee matters, would not be served by dismissal. Defendant as an attorney was sophisticated and knew of his right to arbitrate. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT F. RICHARD II VS. ROBERT F. RICHARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 17, 2010, line 12, Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction and Temporary Restraining Order. Is granted. Court adopts plaintiff's proposed Order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING VS. TIM GOMES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 17, 2010, line 13, DEFENDANT TIM GOMES DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. The 3 day notice is defective because it does not demonstrate grounds under the San Francisco Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Ordinance to evict defendant. See Gross vs. The Superior Court of the City and County of San Francisco (1985) 171 Cal.App. 3d 265. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GRT INVESTMENTS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. WE PARK, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 17, 2010, line 14, DEFENDANT WE PARK, INC. Motion to Quash. Is denied. Even if 30 day notice to pay or quit is improper, defendant fails to show all causes of action are defective. =(302/CWW)
